summary: Matched most of the remaining Game & Watch rescue item functions in itgamewatchrescue.c, replacing placeholder markers with C implementations for lifecycle, animation, state-change, and event callback helpers. The PR also tightened the corresponding header prototypes by naming parameters and replacing an UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototype for it_802C8208 with a concrete void(HSD_GObj*, enum_t) signature. it_802C8038 was explicitly left unmatched because it was larger and expected to take more work.
what_changed: Implemented it_802C8158, which clears the rescue item's stored Game & Watch rescue owner pointer, conditionally calls ftGw_SpecialHi_ItemRescueSetNULL when the item owner matches the stored gobj, clears item->owner, and calls Item_8026A8EC.
Implemented it_802C81C8 as a thin wrapper around it_8026B724.
Implemented it_802C81E8 as a thin wrapper around it_8026B73C.

Implemented it_802C8208 using Item_80268E5C(hsd_gobj, msid, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE) followed by Item_802694CC(hsd_gobj), and updated its header prototype from unknown parameters to concrete HSD_GObj* and enum_t parameters.

Implemented itGamewatchrescue_UnkMotion1_Anim with rescue-removal checks via ftGw_SpecialHi_ItemCheckRescueRemove, owner/pointer cleanup, Item_8026A8EC, and a false return when the rescue item remains active.
Implemented it_2725_Logic81_EvtUnk as a thin wrapper around it_8026B894(item_gobj1, item_gobj2).
Added required includes for baselib forward declarations, item inlines, item APIs, ftGameWatch SpecialHi APIs, and it_26B1.
Updated itgamewatchrescue.h parameter names for several item callbacks and replaced the unknown it_802C8208 declaration with a concrete typed declaration.
smart_moves: Left it_802C8038 as a placeholder instead of forcing a weak match; the PR body notes that the function is large and may take longer.
Used existing item helper routines for simple callback behavior rather than inlining unknown logic: it_8026B724, it_8026B73C, Item_80268E5C, Item_802694CC, Item_8026A8EC, and it_8026B894.
Recognized that the rescue item stores a related gobj in item->xDD4_itemVar.bombhei.xDD8 and consistently clears both that field and item->owner during teardown paths.
Encoded a stack-padding tactic in itGamewatchrescue_UnkMotion1_Anim by declaring unused Item* locals with a comment that they are needed for a match.
Improved header searchability and type safety by naming parameters and replacing unknown prototype macros where the matched implementation revealed concrete types.
decomp_lessons: For item callbacks, very small functions may be exact wrappers around shared item subsystem helpers; checking for wrapper structure can quickly eliminate placeholders.
When a matched implementation reveals a concrete signature, update the header at the same time to remove UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and add meaningful parameter names.

Owner cleanup paths can require both notifying the owning fighter subsystem and clearing item-side references; here ftGw_SpecialHi_ItemRescueSetNULL is only called when the stored rescue gobj matches item->owner.
Stack layout can matter for matching even when locals are unused; this PR documents unused Item* locals as intentional padding for itGamewatchrescue_UnkMotion1_Anim.
It is acceptable and useful to split progress by matching smaller surrounding callbacks while leaving a larger constructor/spawn-style function such as it_802C8038 for later work.
naming_conventions: Item gobj parameters were named item_gobj, item_gobj1, and item_gobj2 in both implementation and header prototypes.
Generic HSD object parameters were named hsd_gobj where the function accepts HSD_GObj* rather than Item_GObj*.
The motion-state id parameter in it_802C8208 was named msid and typed as enum_t.
Existing placeholder-style symbol names were preserved for unmatched or still-unknown functions, including it_802C8038 and it_2725_Logic81_EvtUnk.
The known animation callback kept the project’s item motion naming pattern: itGamewatchrescue_UnkMotion1_Anim.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Declared unused locals item3 through item7 in itGamewatchrescue_UnkMotion1_Anim specifically to pad the stack for a match.
Used repeated GET_ITEM(item_gobj) calls into separate temporaries rather than collapsing them, likely preserving original compiler behavior and register/stack structure.
Used a goto label named end in itGamewatchrescue_UnkMotion1_Anim to structure the false-return path after the non-removal case.
Kept explicit NULL checks on item_gobj, Item*, stored gobj pointers, and owner comparisons instead of simplifying them away.
Matched simple functions as direct tail-like wrappers around existing item helper functions.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.evidence: Only issue comment shown in the PR context; no review comments were present.

follow_up_queries: Investigate whether xDD4_itemVar.bombhei.xDD8 is the correct semantic union member for Game & Watch rescue or just a reused item variable layout.
Look for similar rescue item owner cleanup patterns in other ftGameWatch SpecialHi item code.
Replace remaining placeholder or unknown prototypes near Game & Watch rescue once exact signatures are known.
